strengths: Strong SQL and Python ETL experience
Proven A/B testing and experimentation execution
weaknesses: No PhD or equivalent research degree
Limited distributed systems depth beyond Snowflake/BigQuery
improvement_bullets: Designed and executed A/B tests for digital marketing campaigns, utilizing statistical modeling and causal inference techniques to isolate variable impact and optimize marketing spend.
Built Python-based analytical systems to evaluate AI-powered features, focusing on metric tracking and statistical significance to ensure product-market fit.
Integrated machine learning principles into sales forecasting models, identifying customer segments to drive revenue growth while maintaining a rigorous, research-oriented approach to data quality.
Leveraged dbt and BigQuery to architect scalable data systems, applying advanced analytical frameworks to support cross-functional machine learning initiatives and data-driven product decisions.

Collaborated with cross-functional product teams to define success metrics for new features, applying experimental design to translate raw user data into actionable insights and strategic recommendations.
cover_letter: Dear Hiring Manager, I am excited to submit my application for the Data Scientist, Core Data - PhD (2026) opportunity with Figma. I am interested in this opportunity because of Figma's commitment to building the foundational platforms that empower teams to design and collaborate in real-time. What interests me about working for Figma is its unique approach to embedding AI into the creative workflow, which inspires me because of my own passion for building systems that make data-driven decision-making more accessible. I believe my expertise in rigorous experimentation and my ability to build scalable data pipelines would make me a strong candidate for this role. During my professional career, I have approached problems with a focus on statistical integrity and actionable impact. In fact, while at Shine Masters, I designed and executed A/B tests for digital marketing campaigns, applying statistical modeling to isolate audience response and providing the evidence needed to optimize spend, which resulted in a 300% increase in revenue for targeted segments. Ultimately, I know that the Data Scientist, Core Data - PhD (2026) role at Figma would allow me to bring my experimentation framework skills to help craft how you measure AI-powered features. Additionally, my role as a Data Analyst at Shine Masters enabled me to develop my data infrastructure skills. I architected a production-grade ELT pipeline in Python and SQL, utilizing dbt and BigQuery to ensure data quality and reliability. By automating data validation with Great Expectations and building robust star schemas, I ensured that business stakeholders had a 'single source of truth' for strategic decisions. I am excited by the opportunity to use my background in causal inference and machine learning applications in working on the foundational platforms at Figma. I know that my analytical rigor and technical execution align with what it takes to succeed at the Data Scientist, Core Data - PhD (2026) role. I would love the opportunity to discuss the role in more detail. Thank you for your time and consideration. I look forward to hearing from you soon. Warm regards, Justin Miller
